calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-14-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jeff Holman for Boston Private Bank & Trust. Giulio Accornero for 40 Paternal Heirs.

The Court stated that it does not feel that the issue that Judge Lawrence Kay is a named beneficiary affects her ability to be fair and impartial to any Party, Beneficiary or Counsel. Mr. Holman addressed the issue of fees, explained services provided, and stated that the $200,000.00 reserve amount came out of concern that there is somebody else that would come out of the wood work claiming to be an Heir and that it was a matter of caution. Mr. Accornero stated that it was his understanding that all of the Maternal Heirs were known. The Petition for Final Distribution was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-13-2014 Continued To Jan-21-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-11-2013 Continued To Jan-13-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-27-2013 Continued To Dec-11-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (515 20th Avenue, San Francisco, Ca 94121)
Off Calendar Apr-18-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jeffrey Holman for Borel Private Bank represented by Vern Adkins.

The Court reviewed a copy of proof of publication, instructed Mr. Holman to file the original proof of publication and advised Mr. Holman that the order will be signed until the original is signed. The sale was confirmed to Luis Moran on behalf of Court House Ventures at $960,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale filed March 28, 2013. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). No additional bond is required.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Nancy Bautista/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (515 20th Avenue, San Francisco, Ca 94121)
Off Calendar Apr-10-2013 Continued To Apr-17-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jeffrey Holman for and with Vern Adkins (Representative from Borel Private Bank).

The Court raised the issue that publication is required. Mr. Holman presented oral argument to support his position with regard to the above. The Court waived notice to purchasers, but still requires proper publication pursuant to Probate Code 10308. The Petition to Confirm Sale was continued to April 17, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for publication.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Nancy Bautista. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jul-02-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jeffrey Holman for Borel Private Bank (Petitioner) by Court Call. Jonathan Ferdon for San Francisco Public Administrator (SFPA).

Mr. Ferdon stated that he does not get the examiner's notes and he does not know why the SFPA will have priority if the family members have nominated the Petitioner. Mr. Homan addressed the issues raised in the Examiner's notes with regard to paternal grandparents' heirs. The nominees of issue of grandparents do not have priority under Probate Code 8465(c). Mr. Ferdon did not assert priority for SFPA. The Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administratiion (subject to review of the order). The Administrator is a corporate fiduciary is bond is not required.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jun-18-2012 Continued To Jul-02-12 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
